findings.title: Wing FTP Server Path Disclosure Added to CISA KEV — Actively Chained for RCE
findings.summary: CISA added CVE-2025-47813 (Wing FTP Server path disclosure) to the KEV catalog on 2026-03-16 after confirming attackers are actively chaining it with the critical RCE flaw CVE-2025-47812 to achieve full remote code execution against 10,000+ customer environments including US Air Force and Sony.

findings.title: Two Chrome Zero-Days (Skia + V8) Added to CISA KEV — Targeted Exploitation Confirmed
findings.summary: Google patched two actively exploited Chrome zero-days — CVE-2026-3909 (Skia heap corruption) and CVE-2026-3910 (V8 type confusion) — both added to CISA KEV on 2026-03-13 with confirmed nation-state targeted exploitation prior to patch release.

findings.title: GlassWorm ForceMemo — Mass Force-Push Supply Chain Attack Compromises Hundreds of Python Repos
findings.summary: GlassWorm ForceMemo uses stolen GitHub tokens to force-push crypto-stealing malware into hundreds of Python repos, turning pip install or clone into a silent infection vector.

findings.title: CVE-2025-33073 — NTLM Reflection SMB Exploit Hits 658 GitHub Stars
findings.summary: A public exploit for CVE-2025-33073, an NTLM reflection flaw in Windows SMB, has accumulated 658 GitHub stars, enabling credential capture and relay attacks against Active Directory environments and indicating likely mass scanning or active operator adoption.

findings.title: Rocket.Chat Authentication Bypass — CVE-2026-28514 and CVE-2026-30833
findings.summary: GitHub Security Lab published a two-CVE authentication bypass chain (CVE-2026-28514 and CVE-2026-30833) in Rocket.Chat that allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass login controls and gain unauthorized access to the widely deployed open-source collaboration platform.

findings.title: DRILLAPP — Laundry Bear Deploys Edge-Abusing Backdoor Against Ukrainian Entities
findings.summary: Laundry Bear's DRILLAPP JavaScript backdoor abuses Edge in headless mode to exfiltrate files, audio, webcam, and screen data from Ukrainian orgs via WebSocket C2, with no binary dropped to disk.

findings.title: Stryker Wiper Attack — Tens of Thousands of Devices Destroyed, No Malware Used
findings.summary: Handala wiper against Stryker confirmed destroyed tens of thousands of devices via LOLBAS only — no malware binary deployed, no forensic trail, scope far exceeding initial 5,000-worker estimate.

noted.summary: Pre-auth RCE in React Server Components 19.x added to CISA KEV Dec 2025; China-nexus groups are actively exploiting in ongoing wave with 1,356+ GitHub PoC stars — pre-dates 30-day window but exploitation escalating.
C stack overflow on deeply nested XML content model definitions; widely embedded in Python, Firefox, Apache; no PoC or KEV.
Remote authentication bypass (CISA KEV, previously reported 2026-03-07); new batch scanning PoC tool released within last 24 hours indicating active mass-scanning.
Multiple vulnerabilities (command injection, LPE, unsigned patches, weak crypto) disclosed via Full Disclosure on March 12; niche enterprise product.
Five CVEs including command injection, LPE, unsigned patch distribution, and weak crypto disclosed via Full Disclosure on March 12.
17 vulnerabilities, 6 CVEs (CVSS 9.3) in DeepLink+JSBridge attack chain enabling silent GPS exfiltration; JSBridge patterns transferable to other mobile platforms.
Security updates released March 11 for legacy iOS versions addressing prior vulnerabilities; users on unsupported hardware should apply immediately.
SPN Unicode Collision vulnerability; detection scanner released; insufficient detail from current scan for scoring — monitoring.
Two fresh PoC exploit repos appeared in the last 24 hours with no description available — requires follow-up next cycle.
Four Fortune 500 companies confirmed as victims (Broadcom, Bechtel, Estee Lauder, Abbott) but have not issued public statements; scope of March 2026 Oracle EBS breach continues expanding.
Web SDK supply chain compromise; malicious JavaScript injected to steal cryptocurrency from downstream mobile app analytics integrations.
Active VPN credential theft campaign via phishing infrastructure; no CVE — social engineering vector.
Vulnerability disclosed March 16; insufficient technical detail from current scan for scoring — requires follow-up.
